name: gstack-squad
description: Five-seat operational gstack full-stack team with clearer separation between coordination, planning, building, release, and QA/design.

bootContext.teamDescription: A balanced gstack full-stack team that splits planning, implementation, release, and QA/design into explicit seats while keeping one task-driven flow.
bootContext.initialObjective: Turn unclear requirements into buildable tasks, execute them with disciplined investigation, verify them with real QA/design evidence, and close them with release-grade review and docs sync.
sharedContext: This is a general-purpose full-stack team: each seat owns a direction, but the whole roster covers the end-to-end delivery loop.
Planning and design outputs are only real when reflected back into tasks, subtasks, acceptance criteria, or comments.
Release/review and QA/design are both first-class closure functions; neither should be compressed into an afterthought.
Peer advice is welcome, but work changes must happen through the board and master-controlled routing.
commandChain: user -> master -> task board -> architect-planner -> builder -> qa-lead/reviewer-release -> master -> user
